name: Pot Roast with Mustard Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven, brown the roast at 2-inch intervals with olive oil over medium-high heat. Remove and set aside.
2. Pour off any rendered fat from the roast.
3. Add water, beef broth, wine, garlic, thyme, and black pepper to the pot. Return the roast to the pot.
4. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 18 to 22 minutes per pound, or until a meat thermometer registers 140°F when inserted into the thickest part of the roast.
5. Remove the roast from the pot and let it rest on a serving platter, covered loosely with foil, for about 10 minutes. The internal temperature will rise to 150°F for medium-rare.
6. Strain 1 cup of the poaching liquid from the pot into a small saucepan.
7. Dissolve cornstarch in 1 tablespoon of the strained liquid in the saucepan over medium heat.
8. Gradually add the remainder of the strained liquid, along with mustard and parsley, to the pan containing the cornstarch mixture. Cook and stir until the sauce is clear and slightly thickened, about 3 to 5 minutes.
9. Return the roast to the pot and serve slices of the roast alongside the mustard sauce.
